etymology.description: 䜌 (phonetic) + 心 (heart) -> phonetic and semantic combination relating to emotions -> final form 戀
etymology.interpretation: 戀(love) combines 䜌, which provides the phonetic component, with 心(xīn, 'heart'), symbolizing the emotional or heartfelt aspect of love.
etymology.simplification: 戀(戀) (liàn, "romance; love") was simplified to 恋(恋) by removing the phonetic component 䜌 and retaining the essential meaning of 心 (xīn, "heart").

examples.chinese: 他坠入了深深的恋爱(他墜入了深深的戀愛)。
这首歌充满了浪漫的恋情(這首歌充滿了浪漫的戀情)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shǒu gē chōngmǎnle làngmàn de liànqíng.
examples.english: This song is full of romantic love.

examples.chinese: 在古代诗词中(在古代詩詞中)，恋字常用于表达相思(戀字常用於表達相思)。
examples.pinyin: Zài gǔdài shīcí zhōng, liàn zì cháng yòng yú biǎodá xiāngsī.
examples.english: In ancient poetry, the character 'liàn' was often used to express longing.
